reasoning: The Lean signature encodes each component of the informal claim exactly. `{G : Type*} [Group G]` corresponds to "Let G be a group", `(A : Subgroup G)` corresponds to "A is a subgroup of G", and `(Subgroup.center G) ≤ (Subgroup.normalizer A)` captures "Z(G) ≤ N_G(A)". I confirmed in the local Mathlib that `Subgroup.center G` is the standard center (elements commuting with all of G) and `Subgroup.normalizer A` is the standard normalizer `{g | ∀ n, n ∈ A ↔ g n g⁻¹ ∈ A}`, and `≤` on `Subgroup G` is subset inclusion. No hypotheses are added or dropped and no quantifier structure is changed. This is a strictly faithful formalization.
